new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) in containers of ≤ 2 litres
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.) in Behältnissen mit ≤ 2 Litern
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 21 (excluding sparkling and semi-sparkling wines).
A quality borne by a wine of fresh grapes indicating that it carries a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the Combined Nomenclature.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 68: (1) wine of fresh grapes (subClassOf WineOfFreshGrapes), (2) PDO status (someValuesFrom PDOOriginQuality via RO:has_quality), and (3) container volume ≤ 2 L (someValuesFrom SmallContainer via BFO:has_part). An indicative alcohol-by-volume upper bound (≤ 15% vol., typical for still PDO wines at this code level) is also modelled via a decimalRange restriction. However, the specific alcohol content bands that further subdivide codes 2204 21 11–78 (e.g. ≤ 13%, > 13% ≤ 15%, > 15% ≤ 18%) cannot be determined from the provided legal note text alone — the note refers only to Additional Note 6(a) of Chapter 22 without specifying the precise ABV bracket for code 22042168. Additionally, within 2204 21 the note covers the range 2204 21 11–78, and the specific discriminator for 22042168 versus its siblings (likely actual alcoholic strength) is not stated in the provided text. Coverage is therefore partial.
